line: Baptist. Rev. E. D. Simons, pastor; location, Second Street corner of San Antonio. Pastor's residence, 439 Second Street near the Church. This Church is capable of accommodating about two hundred and fifty persons; regular services on the Sabbath at eleven o'clock A.M. and at half past seven P.M. About one hundred and sixty children attend the Sabbath School, attached to this Church, under the superintendence of Mr. F. E. Adams. Number of teachers, sixteen. Number of volumes in Sabbath School Library, four hundred.

Methodist Episcopal Church. Rev. W. J. Maclay, pastor; location, 290 Second Street between St. John and Santa Clara. This is a new Church, erected during the past year, to supply the place of the one burned in February last. This Church is capable of accommodating about seven hundred persons; regular services on the Sabbath at eleven o'clock A. M., and at half past seven P.M. The Sabbath School attached to this Church has an average attendance of about one hundred and seventy-five scholars, under the superintendence of Mr. C. C. Stephens.

Methodist Episcopal Church-German. Rev. C. H. Afflerbach, pastor. Regular services at present held in the City Hall, at a quarter before eleven o'clock A.M., and at seven o'clock P.M. Sabbath School attached to this Church, at nine o'clock in the morning, numbering about one hundred and twenty-five scholars. Number of teachers, twelve; superintendent, Mr. F. Kuchenbeiser. Number of volumes in Sabbath School Library, five hundred. This society is building a Church at 362 Third Street, which is expected to accommodate a congregation of about three hundred.

Meeting House, Denomination of Friends. Location, St. John Street cor Ninth. Regular services on the Sabbath at eleven o'clock A.M. Sabbath School at ten o'clock A.M.; J. Hobson, teacher. Number of scholars, fifteen. Small library.

Presbyterian Church. Rev. W. Alexander, pastor; location, 271 Second Street. Pastor's residence, 144 First. This Church is capable of accommodating about five hundred persons. Regular services every Sabbath morning at eleven o'clock, and evening at half past seven o'clock. Attached to this Church is a flourishing Sabbath School, numbering about two hundred scholars, under the superintendence of Mr. George H. Hare. Number of volumes in Sabbath School Library, six hundred.

St. Phillip's Episcopal Mission (Colored). Rev. P. W. Cassey, Missionary in charge. Services at Phenixonian Hall, San Antonio Street cor Third, at eleven o'clock A.M., and at five o'clock P.M. Sunday School, at four o'clock P.M. Number of scholars, thirty-eight; number of teachers, five; Jacob Overton, superintendent.

Roman Catholic Church. Rev. D. Kenny, pastor; Rev. Antonio Goets, first assistant, and Rev. B. Picardo (Spanish), second assistant. Location, San Fernando Street cor Market. The building is capable of accommodating about six hundred persons. Regular services, daily at half past six o'clock A.M.; Sunday, at six and half past six o'clock A.M., and at nine o'clock A.M., (for Spanish); and at half past ten o'clock A.M., and at half past seven o'clock P.M., (for English). Sunday School, at two o'clock. Number of scholars, forty, in charge of the pastor.

Trinity Church, Episcopal. Rev. E. S. Peake, rector; location, cor St. John and Second Streets. This Church seats about three hundred persons. Regular services, on the Sabbath, at eleven o'clock A.M., and at seven o'clock P.M. Sunday School, with about seventy scholars, is held every Sabbath at two o'clock P.M., in the Church; Rev. E. S. Peake, superintendent. Number of volumes in Sunday School Library, three hundred.

Zion Methodist Episcopal Church (Colored). Rev. A. Stevens, pastor; location, San Antonio Street cor Fourth. Regular services, on the Sabbath, at half past eleven o'clock A.M., and at seven o'clock P.M.

line: SAN JOSE has one Tribe in a very flourishing condition. Its officers and name are as follows: OSCEOLA TRIBE NO 21 (IMPROVED ORDER OF RED MEN).- Henry Slocum, S.; Lewis Schoen, S.S.; Theo. C. Winchell, J.S.; J. Y. Brown, P.; E. P. Rowe, C. of R.; D. C. Bailey, K. of W. Its stated Councils are held every Tuesday evening.

SAN JOSE GERMAN CLUB, SOCIAL. - Organized 1854. Meetings four times a week at the Club's Hall, 284 Santa Clara Street, (Balbach's Building) with an active membership of about forty. William Fisher, President; H. Stover, Vice President; A. T. Herrmann, Secretary; R. Wise, Treasurer; Charles Otter, Steward and Librarian.

Young Men's Christian Association. Location, rooms at 380 First Street, up stairs. Free reading-room, with library of about eleven hundred volumes. Rooms open every day from twelve o'clock M. to nine o'clock P.M. Prayer meetings, Sunday evenings, at six o'clock. Regular monthly meeting, third Tuesday in each month. Any male member, in good standing, of any Evangelical Church, may become an active member, upon payment of two dollars initiation fee and fifty cents per month dues. Any person of good moral standing may become an associate member, upon payment of three dollars annually. Twenty-five dollars constitute one a life member.
